# Magicborn: The Rune Path — Decisions

| Id | Status | Summary | Consequence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `MRP-SERIES-POSITION` | `accepted` | **In-world order** ([`BOOKS-WORLD-CHRONOLOGY`](/docs/books/planning/decisions)): *Tale* → *Rune Path* → *Legacy*. This book is **not** Tale's immediate sequel; it is **Kael's prequel** before *Legacy*. Shelf: **Rune Path** / Kael prequel — not "Book 3." | Chronology + reading order match; see [section State — Magicborn line](/docs/books/planning/state#magicborn-line-world-order-and-chapter-beats). |
| `MRP-PREQUEL-END` | `accepted` | Final page of the prequel: Kael is **still running** — fugitive status on the **Path**, **not** reconciled back into the Host, **not** "safe." | Shapes Act III landing; gap to Legacy **42** is **off-page ellipsis** until [`mrp-outline-02-02`](/docs/books/magicborn-rune-path/planning/task-registry). |
| `MRP-TIMELINE` | `accepted` | Prequel **opens** at **17**. **Kael is 42** at first on-page **Alun** meeting in [Legacy](/docs/books/mordreds-legacy/planning/state) (**~25 years** later). **Gap** may include off-page history (capture, return, amnesty, etc.) — **must not** contradict **Legacy** Kael without explicit retcon pass. | Works with **`MRP-PREQUEL-END`**: "running" at prequel close ≠ identical to every year in the gap. |
| `MRP-STREAM-SCOPE` | `accepted` | **Magicborn: The Rune Path** is **Kael's** journey out of **Tarro servitude** and through a **clandestine Magicborn network**; not a retread of Mordred's plot. | Tone = survival travel + institutional horror + **craft progression**. |
| `MRP-CONCEIT` | `accepted` | **Rune Path** = decentralized escape route: **wayfinding**, **helpers**, and **runes carved into the world** so later fugitives can **activate** teachings or one-shot aid. Analogous in *role* to the historical **Underground Railroad**; **not** a copy of real names or events — original cultures, geography, and magic rules. | Worldbuilding must show **why carving is worth the risk** and how Tarro **counters** it. |
| `MRP-KAEL-POV` | `accepted` | Primary POV **Kael**; tight third, past tense unless a future experiment is decided. **Legacy** shows the same man at **42** — voice may be **colder** there; prequel earns the distance. | Voice = bureaucratic military training cracking under moral pressure, then Path-forged. |
| `MRP-CARVED-RUNE-RULES` | `proposed` | Carved runes require **intent + medium + reader's voice**; **fade** or **corrupt** if neglected; **House sniffers** exist (tools or specialist mages). | Sets stakes for every mark Kael leaves or follows. |
| `MRP-INSPIRATION-CREDIT` | `accepted` | In **author-facing** planning only, we name **Harriet Tubman** and the **Underground Railroad** as **ethical-historical inspiration** for *structure* (network, courage, no single hero); the novel remains **fantasy** with its own history. | Any **in-world** mention uses **original** metaphors (Rune Path, conductors unnamed or in-world titles). |
| `MRP-DUNGEON-RELIC-LAW` | `accepted` | Magicborn on dungeon duty **draw relics from the armory** by roster slip; **return or reconcile** within statute or face **capital penalty**; in-world text: [`MRP-LAW-DUNGEON-RELIC-CUSTODY`](/docs/magicborn/in-world/magicborn-rune-path/statute-tarro-dungeon-relic-custody). | Ch1 shows **checkout**, **carriage**, and **interior clearance** culture before escape arc. |

## Decision Rule

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| `id-shape` | `MRP-` prefix |
| `status-set` | `accepted`, `leaning`, `proposed`, `superseded` |

| `series` | **Prequel** to *Legacy*; **in-world** runs **after** *Tale*, **before** *Legacy* ([`BOOKS-WORLD-CHRONOLOGY`](/docs/books/planning/decisions)); ends **still running** ([`MRP-PREQUEL-END`](/docs/books/magicborn-rune-path/planning/decisions)) |
| `status` | `outlining` — Ch1 (sortie) drafted |
| `genre` | Political dark fantasy / survival journey — **Magicborn** underground network, cross-border flight, pedagogy through **carved runes** |
| `premise` | **Kael** at **17**: enlisted **Magicborn Host**, **Tarro dungeon** sorties, **armory relic custody** ([statute](/docs/magicborn/in-world/magicborn-rune-path/statute-tarro-dungeon-relic-custody)). Novel tracks path from that duty toward **escape** and the **Rune Path**. **Same Kael** appears in Legacy at **42** when he meets **Alun** ([`ML-KAEL-AGE-AT-ALUN`](/docs/books/mordreds-legacy/planning/decisions), [`MRP-TIMELINE`](/docs/books/magicborn-rune-path/planning/decisions)). |
| `arc` | Locks in [Decisions](/docs/books/magicborn-rune-path/planning/decisions). Tasks in [Task registry](/docs/books/magicborn-rune-path/planning/task-registry). |

## Timeline anchor (cross-book)

| Milestone | Age / note |
| --- | --- |
| Prequel **opening** (current Ch1) | Kael **17** |
| **Legacy** first meeting with **Alun** | Kael **42** |
| **Gap** (~25 years) | **Open** — prequel may not cover all on-page; must not contradict Legacy Kael's **legal fluency**, **armor role**, or **interior choices** once gap is filled ([tasks](/docs/books/magicborn-rune-path/planning/task-registry)) |

## Core conceit (working)

| Element | Intent |
| --- | --- |
| **Rune Path** | Not a single road — a **network** of marks, people, and trial-and-error corridors out of Tarro's reach. |
| **Carved spells** | Runes **cut or burned** into trees, stone, bridge pilings, barn beams — latent until another Magicborn **speaks or feeds** them; teach techniques, warnings, or one-shot escapes. |
| **Pedagogy** | The Path **trains** as it moves you: each leg expects slightly more skill than the last, or punishes hubris. |
| **Risk** | Marks can be **found by the House**; helpers can be **turned**; wrong syllables **burn** the learner. |

## Cast (opening)

| Role | Note |
| --- | --- |
| **Kael** | POV; **17** at Ch1; later **42** in Legacy — see timeline table |
| **Conductors / anchors** | Working names TBD — people and places that move fugitives without keeping lists |
| **Antagonist pressure** | Tarro pursuit, foreign crowns, and **counter-magic** that hunts carved runes |

## Act shape (working)

| Act | Focus |
| --- | --- |
| **I** | Barracks / armory / dungeon clearance → moral break → first marks; learning the rules of the Path |
| **II** | Crossing borders; kingdom-specific law and cruelty; Kael's power deepens |
| **III** | Cost of the network — betrayals, scrubbed marks, choice to **carve for others**; **landing** must leave room for **Legacy-era** Kael ([`MRP-TIMELINE`](/docs/books/magicborn-rune-path/planning/decisions)) |

# Mordred's Legacy — Decisions

| Id | Status | Summary | Consequence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ML-SERIES-ORDER` | `accepted` | **Book 1** = *Tale*; **Book 2** = *Legacy* (this stream). **Rune Path** = Kael **prequel** before Legacy. **World + reading order:** *Tale* → *Rune Path* → *Legacy* ([`BOOKS-WORLD-CHRONOLOGY`](/docs/books/planning/decisions)). | [Section State — Magicborn line](/docs/books/planning/state#magicborn-line-world-order-and-chapter-beats). |
| `ML-KAEL-AGE-AT-ALUN` | `accepted` | **Kael** is **42** years old at his **first on-page** meeting with **Alun** (river / arrest / scaffold sequence in Legacy Ch1). | Rune Path prequel must not end with a 42-year-old Kael unless the prequel spans that span on-page; see [`MRP-TIMELINE`](/docs/books/magicborn-rune-path/planning/decisions) for gap handling. |
| `ML-STREAM-SCOPE` | `accepted` | **Mordred's Legacy** is **not** Book 1's plot retold; it is **institutional**: law, military Magicborn, families, dungeon duty, coalition **after** hardening. | Tone = bureaucracy + intimacy; cite Book 1 decisions only as history. |
| `ML-POV-NAME` | `leaning` | Child POV working name **Alun** — rename freely before prose lock. | [State](/docs/books/mordreds-legacy/planning/state) cast table. |
| `ML-OPENING-RIVER-MAGIC` | `accepted` | Inciting incident: **age ~4**, river with father, **speech** triggers **water** (bubbles / play); father's reaction is **violence + flight**; **not** accidental harm. | Sets "first unlicensed display" legally. |
| `ML-KAEL-ARMOR` | `accepted` | **Kael** and escort: **dark metal** armor with **bark** patches; weapons also carry **bark** — visual continuity with Tarro suppression tech / church issue. | Iconography for Magicborn military. |
| `ML-PARENT-EXECUTION` | `accepted` | Both parents **hanged** publicly after arrest; scaffold scene is **pedagogical horror** paired with Kael's **legal** explanation. | No off-screen mercy for opening act. |
| `ML-FATHER-SILENCE` | `accepted` | At reunion-in-custody, father **looks away**; mother **looks back** — asymmetry of guilt / love. | Emotional hook for Alun's arc. |
| `ML-POV-STICKINESS` | `proposed` | Acts **II–III**: **Alun-only** tight third vs **occasional** adult (e.g. Kael) POV chapters for institutional counterweight. | Affects pacing and spoiler control for Kael's interiority vs prequel. |

## Decision Rule

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| `id-shape` | `ML-` prefix |
| `status-set` | `accepted`, `leaning`, `proposed`, `superseded` |

## Book 2 act scaffold (working)

| Act | Span | Intent | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **I** | Opening → scaffold | River magic → violence → arrest → road → camp glimpse → carriage reunion → **public hangings**; establish **state vs family** and Kael's **statute voice** | **Drafted** — Ch1 manuscript (`01-chapter-1-alun`) |
| **II** | Camp / training / polity | Alun inside **Magicborn** childhood pipeline; **hierarchy**, **church-crown** pressure, **dungeon-adjacent** duty or spectacle; Kael as **recurring** adult presence (duty rotation, not adoptive parent unless revised); midpoint — Alun **breaks or obeys** a rule that exposes **power as inventory** | **Outline** — beats in [task registry](/docs/books/mordreds-legacy/planning/task-registry) |
| **III** | Escalation → endgame | **Convergence** with fallout from Book 1 world (crowns, faith, dungeons); Alun's **agency** vs **asset** status; Kael **cornered** by orders vs memory of his own path; climax + landing **TBD** (survival ≠ freedom; optional echo of **Rune Path** lore as rumor or forbidden mark) | **Outline** — beat sheet pending |

## Opening sequence (scene spine) — Ch1

| Beat | Content |
| --- | --- |
| `1` | **Domestic idyll:** couple + baby; **happiness** on the page (contrast weapon). |
| `2` | **Age ~4, river:** father and child bathing / playing; child **speaks**; **water answers** (bubbles rise — playful). |
| `3` | Father **horrified**; child runs to share joy → **hug**; father **kicks** him down; **abandons** him at the river. |
| `4` | Child returns home; through door sees father **beating** mother. |
| `5` | **Kael** + two Magicborn at door; **arrest** both parents — report of **unlicensed casting** seen at river; they were **nearby**, saw child returning **upset**. |
| `6` | **Bonding road:** Kael + child; **vague** exposition (what Magicborn means, what law is — age-appropriate dread). |
| `7` | **Military camp:** other **children** in **armor**, training — harshness alien to Alun's prior life. |
| `8` | **Memory:** good memories of father; **cognitive dissonance** — wants explanation, reunion. |
| `9` | **Arrival:** carriage stops; parents **also** disembarked in custody; child calls father; father **looks away**; mother **looks back** while dragged. |
| `10` | **Public scaffold:** line of condemned; **nooses**; **both hanged**; Kael **voices statute** — Magicborn (and those who hide them) **do not get normal family law**. |

## Thematic spine

| Thread | Intent |
| --- | --- |
| `state vs love` | The state's definition of **child** and **family** overrides affection the moment power surfaces. |
| `armor as childhood` | Training children as **weapons-in-waiting** vs the river's innocent play. |
| `church-crown-Magicborn triangle` | Book 1 coalition **hardened** into **bureaucracy**; Legacy explores **who benefits**. |
| `kael's two lives` | Adult reader (optional) can read **Legacy** Kael against **Rune Path** young Kael — same statute tongue, different cost. |

| **Tarro** | **Very large**; **massive stump**; dungeon sorties are **duty** |
| **Tarro dungeon** | Popular name for the **Pantheon lair / regrowing tree-root** complex |
| **Elsewhere** | Other kingdoms have **smaller dungeons**; **none like Tarro** |

# Mordred's Tale — Decisions

| Id | Status | Summary | Consequence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `MT-STYLE-DIALOGUE-FIRST` | `accepted` | Newer pages favor dialogue and interiority over long scenic description; enough setting to orient | Each page should have at least one exchange or reaction and one moment of interiority |
| `MT-SANDERSON-ADJACENT` | `accepted` | Tone and structure lean Sanderson-like: clear cause-effect, character-driven choices, propulsive plot | Magic and power feel rule-bound; character goals and costs stay visible |

## Chapter Done Checklist (Definition of Done)

| Check | Pass Criteria |
| --- | --- |
| `1-beats-landed` | All required roadmap beats for the chapter are present in scene form (not notes). |
| `2-scene-causality` | Each scene clearly causes the next or creates pressure for the next. No orphan scene. |
| `3-character-motive` | POV character motive is explicit in each scene; decisions are understandable even when flawed. |
| `4-canon-consistency` | Timeline, magic rules, and named-character facts match decisions and state roadmap. |
| `5-act-handoff` | Chapter ending creates a clean handoff into the next chapter/act objective. |
| `6-dialogue-interiority-balance` | Chapter includes dialogue and interior reaction beats; no long unreadable exposition blocks. |
| `7-placeholder-policy` | No `TODO` placeholders except wording-level polish notes. Missing plot beats are not allowed. |
| `8-read-through` | One full read-through complete; awkward transitions and obvious logic gaps fixed. |
| `9-build-check` | `pnpm run build:books` passes after chapter save when applicable. |

| `BOOKS-HOMEPAGE-READER-LAZY-MOUNT` | `superseded` | homepage reader mounted only when the user explicitly asked for it | replaced by the homepage launch-surface approach |
| `BOOKS-HOMEPAGE-READER-BYTE-LOAD` | `accepted` | embedded reader should load the `.epub` archive into memory before mounting `react-reader` | runtime behavior does not depend on browser MIME handling or URL-style EPUB fallback |
| `BOOKS-HOMEPAGE-LAUNCHPAD` | `accepted` | homepage is the launch surface for books and routes into `/apps/reader*book=<slug>`; legacy **`/books/**` pages removed** with **308** to `/apps/reader` (static EPUB files stay under `/books/<slug>/book.epub`) | the reader app route is the canonical live reading runtime |
| `BOOKS-DEDICATED-READER-BOOK-SWITCHER` | `accepted` | active reading sessions should expose book switching in the read-page header | readers can move between available books without leaving the reader route |
| `BOOKS-DEDICATED-READER-SPREADS` | `accepted` | wide screens should use paginated two-page spreads when the viewport allows it | the reading surface can feel like an opened book as long as each emitted page shell stays compact enough to fit |
| `BOOKS-DEDICATED-READER-TOC-STYLING` | `accepted` | TOC chrome and in-book TOC typography should feel designed, not default | navigation surfaces should look intentional even before content-specific polish |

The host passes **`renderPlanningCockpit`** (e.g. **`RepoPlannerCockpitClient`**) into **`ReaderWorkspace`** | [embed architecture](/docs/documentation/reader-repo-planner-embed-architecture); reader cockpit works without site **`ModalRoot`** for strip-driven opens |
| `BOOKS-READER-BUILT-IN-SHELF-FIRST` | `accepted` | `/apps/reader` should always present repo-built books as the default library surface, with local EPUB import as a secondary action instead of the empty-state primary experience | the reader route feels like a real library product even before local-file workflows are used |

| `BOOKS-READER-MANIFEST-NEEDS-COVER-METADATA` | `accepted` | the generated books manifest and `BookEntry` model should grow enough metadata to render a shelf cleanly: cover asset path or fallback, author, description, and readiness/progress hints | the library UI should not scrape EPUBs at runtime just to know how to present built-in titles |
| `BOOKS-READER-WORKSPACE-THREE-STATES` | `accepted` | the reader workspace should explicitly model three states: built-in library, built-in reading session, and local-file reading session | runtime exports from `@portfolio/repub-builder` stay clear and host apps do not have to infer shell state from missing query params alone |
| `BOOKS-READER-PLANNING-PACK-FROM-BOOK-ARTIFACT` | `accepted` | for a **repo-built** reading session, **planning pack** data shown in the **Repo Planner cockpit** from the reader should come from **that book's shipped EPUB** (planning supplement appendix emitted via **`repub epub ... --planning`** / per-book **`epubPlanningDirs`**), not from ad hoc **`/docs/...`** links or site **`builtin-packs.json`** | **Reader** cockpit uses **`loadSiteBuiltinPacks={false}`** — **no** site built-in packs there; **`builtin-packs.json`** remains for **`/apps/repo-planner`** and the **global** modal only until book-scoped packs ship (**`books-reader-04`**) |
| `BOOKS-REPUB-ARTIFACT` | `accepted` | we formally call the **portfolio reading bundle** for a title a **repub reading artifact** (informally **repub file**): **EPUB3** built from `books/<slug>/` plus **bundled planning trees** at build time; **`books-reader-02-01`** adds **merged author annotations** into the same pipeline output | one emitted file (or zip) per title is the **source of truth** for **reader workspace** features tied to that book; RichEPub **`.repub`** (`repub build`) remains the **app-in-zip** format and may converge later via manifest |
| `BOOKS-READER-FALLBACK-EMPTY-COVER` | `accepted` | missing built-in cover art should use the existing EmptyCover-style placeholder treatment instead of ad hoc gradients or text-only fallbacks | the shelf can stay visually coherent even when a title has no authored cover asset yet |
| `BOOKS-READER-STATUS-CHIPS-PROGRESS-FIRST` | `accepted` | shelf cards should use simple reading-status chips modeled after Koodo's signals: `New`, percent read when progress exists, `Done`, and `Coming soon` for non-built titles | the shelf communicates usefulness at a glance without inventing a large status taxonomy |
| `BOOKS-READER-LOCAL-IMPORT-SECONDARY` | `accepted` | local EPUB import should remain obvious, but as a utility action/card beside the built-in shelf rather than the primary empty-state experience | repo books stay the first thing users see, while ad hoc reading still remains one click away |
| `BOOKS-AI-COPILOTKIT-V1` | `superseded` | the first public chat shipped on CopilotKit + OpenAI as a read-only assistant | replaced by the assistant-ui shell once citation-backed RAG and bespoke chat chrome became first-class needs |
| `BOOKS-AI-ASSISTANT-UI-SHELL` | `accepted` | the public site chat shell runs on assistant-ui with a local runtime, a thin `/api/chat` backend route, Claude-style modal chrome, and development-only devtools access | the frontend owns the chat UI/state while the backend stays responsible for RAG grounding and OpenAI calls |
| `BOOKS-AI-HARD-CUTOVER` | `accepted` | once the assistant-ui shell shipped, the public chat stack should remove the old CopilotKit route and packages instead of keeping a compatibility layer | there is exactly one public chat path to maintain: assistant-ui + `/api/chat` |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-PAYLOAD-SQLITE-VEC` | `accepted` | planned RAG backend uses Payload CMS over SQLite for sources/chunks/ingest records and `sqlite-vec` for embeddings + KNN search | repo-authored content remains canonical; the DB stores indexed retrieval copies and vectors |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-SIDECAR-INDEX-DB` | `accepted` | Payload keeps canonical RAG records in its own SQLite file, while FTS and `sqlite-vec` live in a sidecar RAG SQLite file | Payload schema introspection stays stable, while the vector/FTS index can load `sqlite-vec` without coupling that extension to the Payload connection |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-CITATIONS-FIRST` | `accepted` | AI answers should be able to expose retrieved chunks with source labels, URLs, and anchors | retrieval payloads must preserve provenance metadata, not just raw chunk text |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-REPO-INGEST` | `accepted` | "know more about me" comes from chunking/indexing repo and other owner-managed source material, not a hand-curated static about-me prompt file | ingest/chunking quality matters more than adding a bespoke author bio document |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-SOURCE-SCOPE` | `accepted` | ingest `content/docs`, `content/projects`, `content/blog`, and books/world-building material; exclude the raw reader-facing manuscript by default | public chat should know the world, characters, and work without treating draft manuscript pages as the main retrieval corpus |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-MAGICBORN-SECTION` | `accepted` | worldbuilding docs live in a dedicated `magicborn` docs section and act as the primary public lore corpus for chat retrieval | world/lore writing has a clear home in the docs IA and does not get conflated with manuscript chapters |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-INGEST-SCRIPT-FIRST` | `accepted` | support both manual/scripted ingest and an owner-only admin trigger, but treat the script path as canonical | indexing stays operable without depending on the admin UI, and admin remains convenience rather than the only workflow |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-SOURCE-PER-PAGE` | `accepted` | create one source record per public page instead of grouping multiple pages into one family document | citations, checksums, and incremental ingest stay simpler and more stable |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-EMBEDDING-TARGET` | `accepted` | default embedding target is OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` at `1536` dimensions with cosine distance | quality stays high for a mixed but modest corpus without prematurely optimizing for storage or latency |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-PAYLOAD-OWNS-CHUNKS` | `accepted` | Payload stores canonical chunk text and citation metadata; the sidecar FTS/`sqlite-vec` DB stores vectors keyed by `chunkId` plus only small filter fields | retrieval can use vector search without duplicating large text blobs in the vec table or forcing vec tables into the Payload DB |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-CHUNK-BODY-STORED` | `accepted` | each chunk record stores its normalized chunk body directly | retrieval, citations, and debugging do not depend on reconstructing text from offsets |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-LAST-COMPLETED-RUN` | `accepted` | chat should only read from the last fully completed ingest run, never a half-rebuilt corpus | reindexing stays operationally simple and avoids mixed old/new retrieval results |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-SOFT-DELETE-AUDIT` | `accepted` | removed sources may remain soft-deleted in Payload for audit, but their active FTS/vector rows should disappear from retrieval as soon as the replacement run commits | admin can retain history without leaking retired content back into chat |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-STABLE-SOURCE-IDS` | `accepted` | source ids stay stable across runs, while chunk ids may be regenerated per run | citations and source links can stay stable even when chunk boundaries shift after edits |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-HYBRID-RERANK` | `accepted` | retrieval should add a lightweight rerank/filter pass after vector search instead of relying on pure top-k | metadata checks plus a small lexical/FTS pass should improve exact-name and title-sensitive queries |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-RERANK-BM25` | `accepted` | the lexical rerank stage should use SQLite FTS5 BM25 over the vector-candidate chunk set, not token overlap only | exact names, titles, and phrase-sensitive queries should recover better without abandoning semantic recall |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-FTS-TEXT-PLUS-METADATA` | `accepted` | FTS should index chunk text plus selected metadata like titles, headings, aliases, and section labels | lexical rerank can recover exact-title and exact-name queries more reliably |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-ANCHORED-CITATIONS` | `accepted` | citations should preserve heading or section anchors when available | source cards can link users to the precise part of a page, not just the page root |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-INCREMENTAL-CHECKSUM-INGEST` | `accepted` | ingest should rebuild only changed sources when checksums differ | v1 stays fast enough to rerun often without full-corpus rebuilds |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-AUDIT-HIDDEN-BY-DEFAULT` | `accepted` | soft-deleted sources and old ingest runs should be hidden in normal admin views by default | the owner UI stays clean while retaining auditability |
| `BOOKS-AI-RAG-CITATION-PANEL` | `accepted` | chat renders source chunks inside a collapsible Sources panel by default | answers stay cleaner while still exposing provenance and snippets on demand |
| `BOOKS-AI-ADMIN-BASIC-AUTH` | `accepted` | Payload admin and ingest/reindex controls use basic auth in production | public chat can stay open/read-only without shipping an unauthenticated admin surface |
| `BOOKS-AI-SCOPED-LOGGING` | `accepted` | client and server AI flows should use one shared logger with env-driven levels and scope filters instead of permanent ad hoc console statements | chat/RAG debugging can become verbose in development and targeted sessions, then be quiet again without deleting instrumentation |
| `BOOKS-AI-STACK-NEXT` | `accepted` | assistant-ui is the active public shell; follow-on evaluations now narrow to Tool UI for schema-first tool rendering and MCP-oriented surfaces only if the assistant becomes more tool-heavy later | avoid parallel public chat stacks; add new UI/runtime layers only when a concrete feature forces them |

## EPUB build: planning supplement

The **`@portfolio/repub-builder`** CLI packs `books/<slug>/` into EPUB. Extra planning material can ride along as **appendix XHTML** in the spine (not listed in the generated table of contents):

| Mechanism | Detail |
| --- | --- |
| CLI | **`repub epub <book-folder>`** (first positional = manuscript / book source tree; there is **no** `--input` flag today) plus repeatable **`--planning <dir>`** for each planning root to embed as appendix. Example: `repub epub ./books/mordreds_tale --planning ./apps/portfolio/content/docs/books/planning --output ./out.epub`. |
| `pnpm run build:books` | Reads optional **`epubPlanningDirs`** in each `book.json` (paths **relative to that book folder**), resolves them, and passes `--planning` for each existing directory. |
| Files included | Recursive `.md`, `.mdx`, `.xml`, `.toml`, `.txt` under each root; skips `node_modules`, `.git`, `dist`, `.next`, `__pycache__`. |
| Built-in titles | Each `books/<slug>/book.json` can list `../../apps/portfolio/content/docs/books/planning` so **books** planning MDX ships inside the built EPUB (shared tree; stream-specific pages live beside section planning). Includes **Mordred's Tale**, **Mordred's Legacy**, **Magicborn: The Rune Path**. |

**Reader + Repo Planner:** the **in-browser cockpit** for a **built-in** book should load **planning pack** material from **this embedded supplement** (and eventual **repub** manifest), not from ad hoc doc URLs — see [embed architecture](/docs/documentation/reader-repo-planner-embed-architecture) (**Book-scoped planning packs**) and decisions **`BOOKS-READER-PLANNING-PACK-FROM-BOOK-ARTIFACT`**, **`BOOKS-REPUB-ARTIFACT`**. Tasks: **`books-reader-04`**.

Verify with **`pnpm run build:books`** and open the output under `apps/portfolio/public/books/<slug>/book.epub`.

**CLI productization** (help UX, **`npx` / global install**, publishing): [documentation task registry](/docs/documentation/planning/task-registry) phase **`documentation-site-07-02`**.

# EPUB planning pack manifest (`books-reader-04-01`)

## Location

- **Path in zip:** `META-INF/portfolio-planning-pack.json`
- **Version field:** top-level `planningPackManifestVersion: 1` (integer). Parsers should reject or best-effort unknown major versions.

## Shape (v1)

```json
{
  "planningPackManifestVersion": 1,
  "entries": [
    {
      "href": "OEBPS/plan-planning-state.xhtml",
      "virtualPath": "docs/books/planning/state.mdx",
      "title": "planning: State",
      "sourceKind": "mdx"
    }
  ]
}
```

| Field | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `href` | Path of the appendix XHTML inside the EPUB zip (matches epub-gen layout under `OEBPS/`). |
| `virtualPath` | Stable path shown in the cockpit file tree; should mirror site embed prefix where possible (`docs/books/planning/...`). |
| `title` | Human title from the appendix header (front matter or file label). |
| `sourceKind` | `md` \| `mdx` \| `raw` — hints how the body was produced; runtime extraction may still yield plain text with HTML lossiness for md/mdx. |

## Virtual path rules (emit)

When a planning root directory lies under `.../content/docs/`, the emitter sets:

`virtualPath = 'docs/' + <path under content/docs, no leading slash> + '/' + <file relative to planning root>`

(e.g. `docs/books/planning/state.mdx`) with forward slashes. Otherwise:

`virtualPath = planning/<basename(planningRoot)>/<relative path>`

## Runtime discovery order (`books-reader-04-02`)

1. **Manifest-first:** if `META-INF/portfolio-planning-pack.json` parses as v1, use its `entries` in order.
2. **Fallback (legacy EPUBs):** zip entries whose path matches `OEBPS/plan-*.xhtml` (case-insensitive), sorted by path. For each file, `virtualPath` is derived from the `.planning-appendix-path` paragraph in the XHTML when present; else `planning/epub/<basename(href)>`.

## Cockpit pack identity

One **`PlanningPack`** (or one embed pack) per catalog title:

- **Id:** `book-<slug>-planning` (slug = host `readingTargetId`, e.g. `mordreds_tale` → `book-mordreds_tale-planning`).

## Content fidelity (v1)

Appendix bodies are **XHTML** in the zip. Extraction for the inspector uses **lossy** plain text: strip tags, decode basic entities, preserve some block breaks. Raw files use `<pre>` content with entity decoding. This is acceptable for v1; richer markdown round-trips are optional later.

## Empty / missing planning

If the book build has **no** `epubPlanningDirs` (or empty trees), the emitter **omits** the manifest file. Runtimes treat that as **no** embedded planning pack.

## Goals

- **Rail:** Library-only by default; optional host links via `readerShellNavLinks`. Brand block (“Reader / Workspace”) navigates to the reader library (same as Library).
- **Shelf:** Built-in titles with a host-provided planning payload show **Planning cockpit** on the card; modal uses the same payload as the in-reader strip (EPUB injection applies only when that book is open in the viewer).
- **Surfaces:** Reader routes use `SidebarInset` `bg-background` so the shell matches site shadcn tokens. Catalog field is `type="text"` + `role="searchbox"` (not `type="search"`) to avoid WebKit’s extra search affordances beside the app’s magnifier icon.
- **Theme:** Reader chrome uses the same Tailwind semantic classes as the rest of the portfolio (`border-border`, `bg-card`, `text-muted-foreground`, etc.). The old **ember / ink** toggle was a separate palette; it has been removed so the reader always follows the site theme.
- **Genres:** `genres` in each book’s `book.json` are copied into `public/books/manifest.json` by `scripts/build-books.cjs` and shown on shelf cards (replacing “Built in” / “Queued” pills).
